title: potentilla × kluckii
intro: Potentilla × kluckii, also known as Kluck's cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species in the family Rosaceae, derived from the cross between Potentilla fruticosa and Potentilla erecta. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits associated with Potentilla × kluckii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal blends.
